title: cedrelopsis ambanjensis
intro: Cedrelopsis ambanjensis, also known as the Tsitugu, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the island of Madagascar. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cedrelopsis ambanjensis include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. In herbal preparations, Cedrelopsis ambanjensis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also applied topically to the skin in the form of creams and ointments.
